texto: it would be fairer to say I have traveled widely, without ever leaving my own native soil, I've traveled, one might say, through literature, each time I've opened a book the pages echoed with a noise like the dip of a paddle in midstream, and throughout my odyssey I never crossed a single border, and so never had to produce a passport, I'd just pick a destination at random, setting my prejudices firmly to one side, and be welcomed with open arms in places swarming with weird and wonderful characters
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alain Mabanckou: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “it would be fairer to say I have traveled widely, without ever leaving my own native soil, I've t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, joy-of-reading, literature pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e literature.
